component.name: CoreMetrics
component.summary: Cross-platform desktop system monitor (CPU / RAM / GPU / processes)

description.p: CoreMetrics is a small C++23 system monitor built directly on raw SDL3 pixel surfaces. It shows live CPU, RAM, GPU, and per-process resource usage with an optional rolling sparklines view and an htop-style kill flow on the Processes tab.

No Qt, no Electron, no JavaScript runtime. Every widget is hand-rasterized by code in this repository. Three native metrics backends (Linux /proc, macOS Mach + IOKit, Windows PDH) are selected at compile time so the same source tree builds the same UI on each platform.

screenshot.caption: System tab with CPU / RAM / GPU bars and per-logical-CPU strip
Sortable Processes table with real per-process CPU%

description.p: Cleanup wave from two parallel review agents: 13 unused COLOR_* constants and a one-liner formatUptime wrapper dropped from coremetrics.cpp; explicit sstream include for formatRate; Screen::drawBox area threshold bumped to 4096 pixels so per-core strip cells, mem-breakdown segments, and 5-column row cells stay on the inline path (ThreadPool dispatch dominates small-box fills).

Network I/O backend (cross-platform readNetIo): getifaddrs on mac, /proc/net/dev on linux, GetIfTable on windows; loopback excluded. System tab gains an aggregate disk-I/O strip on the left and an aggregate net-I/O strip on the right at y=68. Screen::drawBox fast path for boxes <= 1024 pixels (signal menu borders, separators, small overlays) skips the ThreadPool dispatch overhead.

Audit roundup from a 4-agent parallel review wave: --poll-ms parse + clamp centralized in ProcessUtils with full unit coverage (rejects negative-wrap inputs), pointInRect helper for the EXIT-button hit test, sparkline first push pre-fills the ring buffer so polylines span full width from frame 1. README, DOCS, CONTRIBUTING, ADVANTAGES brought back in sync with the v0.2.6 shipped surface and the now-gating Windows CI.

New --poll-ms CLI flag overrides the 500ms refresh cadence (clamped 100..10000 ms); footer label tracks the active value. EXIT button redesigned: bigger, bright red, white centered text instead of dark-red sticker with thin white text. The old 'cores' label on the per-core CPU strip was dropped (collided with the CPU history sparkline label).

System tab decluttered: memory breakdown strip slimmed so it reads as a continuation of the RAM bar rather than a second RAM bar stacked beneath it; per-core CPU strip gains a dim 'cores' label at the left edge in the same style as the CPU / RAM / GPU rows.

Windows CI now gates merges (allow-failure flipped off) after a full stabilization pass: SDL3_ROOT bypass for pkg-config includes, -lpdh -lpsapi for the Windows backend, SDL3 DLL copy next to bin/tests.exe, static C++ runtime, and the .exe suffix invocation. README + API.md aligned with the v0.2.3 5-column Processes table and DISK status row. Load text on the System status row aligned to the y=44 baseline that Up and DISK already shared. Adds scripts/windows-arm64-smoke.ps1 for the manual Win11 ARM UTM live test.

Per-process DISK I/O column on the Processes tab. Throughput formatted as X.X MB/s or XXX KB/s; idle processes show nothing. Column-header click sorts by read+write rate via the new SORT_DISK enum. Layout weights rebalanced to fit 5 columns (NAME loses 8% to make room).

Root volume disk usage on the System tab: 'DISK used/total GB (NN%)' status line with the same yellow-at-60% / red-at-80% color thresholds the RAM bar uses. Cross-platform backend via statvfs on linux and mac, GetDiskFreeSpaceEx on windows.

Post-v0.2.0 polish wave from an automated audit: stuck-at-100% RAM fix on Linux kernels missing MemAvailable, Windows PDH GPU read no longer hits UB on a zero-sample probe, macOS per-process MEM% computed in double, README and DOCS test counts and screenshot caption matched to reality, pure ProcessUtils helpers for I/O rate / filter / uptime with unit tests, live procs counter and sparkline labels in the System hero UI.

htop-style upgrades: process search / filter, parent-child tree view, per-process disk I/O backend, memory breakdown segments, uptime and 1/5/15 load averages, Windows kill signal backend, --tree and --filter CLI flags for headless screenshot capture.
AssetPath resolver, htop-style row selection and kill, per-logical-CPU bar strip, dynamic README contribution badge, install-path badges.
First tagged release: overnight wave + tag-driven release pipeline + Homebrew tap.
